data.body:
CHECKPOINT COMPLETE: Two fixes applied to tests/hooks/test-behavioral-equivalence-allowlist.sh:

1. BASELINE_HASH pre-computation: Moved hash_before computation outside the per-test helper. Was: 1 bash subprocess per file for hash_before × 10-12 tests = 10-12 extra invocations. Now: computed once after git init.

2. Cache collision fix: compute-diff-hash.sh caches by git index mtime (seconds precision). When multiple tests run in <1s, consecutive index staging/unstaging operations share the same mtime → stale cache hits → wrong hashes returned. Fix: pass TMPDIR=<call-unique-dir> for each compute-diff-hash.sh invocation, giving each call an empty cache namespace.

3. WORKFLOW_CONFIG_FILE + WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_ARTIFACTS_DIR: Set at init time to skip git-based config resolution and artifact dir discovery overhead in each invocation.

Result: Test now runs in ~8-26s (consistently) vs. >73s timeout. All 39 assertions pass in 6/6 consecutive runs.
